title: Chrysanthemum helichrysiflorum
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum helichrysiflorum is classified under the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Chrysanthemum, which is a group of flowering plants native to Asia. The species is a perennial herb with yellow or white flowers, often used in traditional medicine. Chrysanthemum helichrysiflorum is also known as the Yellow-flowered Chrysanthemum or Yellow-flowered Dendranthema. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia, specifically in China and Japan.

intro: Chrysanthemum helichrysiflorum, also known as the Chinese chrysanthemum, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum helichrysiflorum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as Chrysanthemum tea, which is often consumed to promote relaxation and relieve respiratory issues.
